verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane immunoglobulin-superfamily protein expressed exclusively on spermatozoa, where it is essential for sperm-egg fusion by binding its oocyte receptor JUNO. At baseline it resides in the acrosomal membrane of sperm and is relocalized/exposed on the sperm plasma membrane surface following the acrosome reaction. Surface display is thus restricted to a narrow germline lineage (mature/acrosome-reacted sperm), which maps to tissue_restricted_surface rather than yes despite the bona fide TM anchor. Not cell-state stress/cancer/death induced; not lysosomal exocytosis; not a stable attachment to a partner; the germline-restricted display dominates the call.
